function.name: filer_create_file
function.description: Create a file in AI workspace

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite if file exists

function.name: filer_read_file
function.description: Read a file (workspace or granted path)

path.description: Relative workspace path or absolute granted path

function.name: filer_list_files
function.description: List files in a directory

path.description: Relative workspace path or absolute granted path (defaults to workspace root)

function.name: filer_update_file
function.description: Update an existing file

path.description: Relative workspace path or absolute granted path

create_backup.description: Whether to create a backup (.bak) before updating

function.name: filer_delete_file
function.description: Delete a file (workspace only)

function.name: filer_move_file
function.description: Move or rename a file within workspace

function.name: filer_copy_file
function.description: Copy a file (can copy from granted paths to workspace)

source.description: Source path (workspace or granted absolute path)

destination.description: Destination path (must be relative workspace path)

function.name: filer_search_files
function.description: Search for files by name or content

query.description: Search query (filename pattern or content text)

search_type.description: Search in filename or file content

function.name: filer_check_grant
function.description: Check if path is accessible

path.description: Absolute path to check access for

function.name: filer_request_grant
function.description: Request access to a path

path.description: Absolute path to request access for

reason.description: Why AI needs access (would be shown to user for approval)

function.name: filer_list_grants
function.description: List all active grants

function.name: filer_get_workspace_info
function.description: Get workspace location and statistics

function.name: filer_get_audit_log
function.description: Get file operation audit log

operation_filter.description: Filter by operation type (e.g., read_file, create_file)

date_from.description: Filter entries from this date (ISO format)

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return

function.name: memory_store_fact
function.description: Store a single fact about the user. Use category: "preference" for likes/dislikes/settings, "background" for personal details (location, job, age), "contact" for people they mention, "project" for things they're working on, "decision" for choices made, "pattern" for recurring behaviors. Before storing, consider if an existing fact should be deleted first to avoid duplicates.

agent_id.description: Which agent is storing this fact

fact.description: A concise, self-contained statement (e.g., "Prefers dark mode", "Lives in London", "Manager is Anna")

category.description: Fact category: preference, background, contact, project, decision, or pattern

source.description: Optional conversation ID that created this fact

function.name: memory_list_facts
function.description: List all stored facts, optionally filtered by category. Use this to review what is known about the user or to find a specific fact ID for deletion. For searching by keyword, use retrieve_memories instead.

limit.description: Maximum number of facts to return

function.name: memory_delete_fact
function.description: Delete a fact by its ID. Use list_facts first to find the ID. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect facts before storing a corrected version.

fact_id.description: The ID of the fact to delete

function.name: memory_update_fact
function.description: Update an existing fact with new information. Use this to supersede outdated facts instead of delete + store. For example, if the user moved from London to Berlin, update the existing "Lives in London" fact rather than creating a duplicate.

fact_id.description: The ID of the fact to update (use list_facts to find it)

fact.description: The new fact text to replace the old one

function.name: memory_retrieve_memories
function.description: Search across both facts and past conversations by keyword. This is the primary memory lookup tool — use it when the user asks "do you remember", "what do you know about", or when you need context before responding. For browsing all facts by category use list_facts. For searching only past chat transcripts with date filters use search_conversations.

limit.description: Maximum number of results per type

include_conversations.description: Whether to include conversations in results

function.name: memory_store_conversation
function.description: Log a conversation exchange (user message + agent response). Automatically extracts and stores facts from the conversation. Called internally after each chat interaction — rarely needs to be called manually.

session_id.description: Optional session ID to group conversations

function.name: memory_search_conversations
function.description: Search past chat transcripts by keyword with optional date range. Searches both user messages and agent responses. Use this when the user asks "what did we talk about last week" or to find a specific past discussion. For searching stored facts instead, use list_facts or retrieve_memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of conversations to return

function.name: memory_get_profile
function.description: Get the user's profile including name, background, preferences, current projects, and learned patterns. Use this to personalize responses or check what structured information is stored about the user.

function.name: memory_update_profile
function.description: Update user profile fields. Supports dot notation for nested fields (e.g., "user_info.name", "preferences.communication"). Use this for structured, long-lived user attributes. For individual facts or learnings, use store_fact instead.

function.name: memory_store_skill
function.description: Create a new autonomous skill — a scheduled or manually triggered behavior the assistant performs independently. trigger_type: "cron" for recurring schedules (e.g., daily email digest), "manual" for on-demand execution, "event" for event-driven triggers. Instructions should be natural language describing what the LLM should do step by step.

description.description: Brief description of what this skill does

trigger_config.description: Scheduling config for cron trigger_type. Supports cron expressions, intervals, one-shot absolute, and one-shot relative formats.

instructions.description: Natural language instructions for the LLM to follow when executing this skill

required_tools.description: List of tool names this skill needs

execution_plan.description: For simple skills: a compiled sequence of tool calls with static params

notify_on_completion.description: Send a Telegram notification when skill finishes (default: true)

notify_interval_minutes.description: Minimum minutes between notifications. 0 = use global default.

enabled.description: Whether the skill is enabled (default: true)

function.name: memory_list_skills
function.description: List all registered skills with optional filtering by enabled status or trigger type. Returns skill IDs, names, schedules, and last run information.

function.name: memory_get_skill
function.description: Get a single skill by ID

function.name: memory_update_skill
function.description: Update an existing skill (enable/disable, change schedule, update instructions, record last run)

skill_id.description: The ID of the skill to update

last_notified_at.description: ISO datetime of last notification sent

function.name: memory_delete_skill
function.description: Delete a skill by ID

skill_id.description: The ID of the skill to delete

function.name: memory_create_contact
function.description: Create a new contact — a person the user works with or knows. Contacts are linked to projects and used to enrich email/calendar context. Duplicate emails within the same agent are rejected.

company.description: Company or organization the contact belongs to

function.name: memory_list_contacts
function.description: List contacts with optional filtering by email, company, type, or name. Use this to look up people by email address (e.g., when processing incoming emails) or to find all contacts at a company.

limit.description: Maximum number of contacts to return (default: 50)

function.name: memory_update_contact
function.description: Update an existing contact. Any provided field will be updated; omitted fields remain unchanged.

contact_id.description: The ID of the contact to update

function.name: memory_create_project
function.description: Create a new project — something the user is working on. Projects are linked to contacts (clients) and used to organize email/calendar context. Duplicate names within the same agent are rejected.

primary_contact_id.description: ID of the primary contact (client) for this project

participants.description: Array of contact IDs for additional people involved

function.name: memory_list_projects
function.description: List projects with optional filtering by status, type, company, contact, or priority. Use contact_id to find all projects a specific person is involved in.

limit.description: Maximum number of projects to return (default: 50)

function.name: memory_update_project
function.description: Update an existing project. Any provided field will be updated; omitted fields remain unchanged.

project_id.description: The ID of the project to update

function.name: memory_query_timeline
function.description: Query what happened across a time range. Searches facts, conversations, profile changes, skill executions, contacts, and projects within the given dates. Use when the user asks "what happened last week", "what was I working on Tuesday", or "show me everything since January".

date_to.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS). Defaults to now.

categories.description: Filter to specific event types. Omit to search all.

query.description: Optional keyword to filter results across all sources

function.name: memory_get_memory_stats
function.description: Get memory usage statistics

agent_id.description: Agent ID to get stats for

function.name: memory_backfill_embeddings
function.description: Generate embeddings for facts that do not yet have them. Finds facts without entries in vec_facts, embeds them in batches, and stores the vectors. Call repeatedly until remaining is 0. Requires an embedding provider to be configured.

agent_id.description: Agent ID to filter facts by

batch_size.description: Number of facts to process per call (default 50)

function.name: memory_backfill_extract_facts
function.description: Process a batch of historical conversations that have never been mined for facts. Finds conversations with no associated facts, runs fact extraction on each, and stores the results. Call repeatedly until remaining is 0.

agent_id.description: Agent ID to filter conversations by

batch_size.description: Number of conversations to process per call (default 10)

function.name: memory_export_memory
function.description: Export memory to human-readable files

function.name: memory_import_memory
function.description: Import user-edited memory files (profile or facts)

file_path.description: Path to the file to import

function.name: memory_synthesize_facts
function.description: Consolidate accumulated facts: merge duplicates, resolve contradictions, flag stale entries. Processes facts per category (max 100 oldest per category for quality). Designed to run weekly via Inngest cron.

agent_id.description: Agent ID to synthesize facts for

max_facts_per_category.description: Maximum facts to process per category (default 100)

function.name: memory_link_project_source
function.description: Link a unified project to an external MCP source. Creates a mapping between a project in the Memorizer and its representation in an external service (e.g., Vercel, PostHog, GitHub).

project_id.description: ID of the unified project to link

external_project_id.description: The project ID as known by the external service

external_project_name.description: The project name as it appears in the external service

function.name: memory_unlink_project_source
function.description: Remove the link between a unified project and an external MCP source.

mcp_name.description: Name of the external MCP to unlink

function.name: memory_list_project_sources
function.description: List linked sources for projects. Filter by project_id to see all MCPs linked to a project, or by mcp_name to see all projects linked to an MCP.

function.name: memory_update_project_source_status
function.description: Update the health status of a project source after checking the external MCP. Used by status check skills.

project_source_id.description: ID of the project source to update

last_checked_at.description: ISO datetime of when the check was performed (defaults to now)

function.name: searcher_web_search
function.description: Search the web using Brave Search

freshness.description: Filter results by recency (24h, day, week, month, year)

function.name: searcher_news_search
function.description: Search news using Brave Search

freshness.description: Filter results by recency (24h, day, week, month)

function.name: searcher_image_search
function.description: Search images using Brave Search

function.name: searcher_web_fetch
function.description: Fetch a URL and extract its content as clean markdown using Mozilla Readability

url.description: The URL to fetch and extract content from

maxLength.description: Maximum characters of extracted content to return (default: 20000)

includeLinks.description: Preserve hyperlinks in markdown output (default: true)

function.name: gmail_list_emails
function.description: List emails from Gmail with optional search query. Supports Gmail search syntax: 'from:john@example.com', 'is:unread', 'subject:meeting', 'newer_than:2d', 'has:attachment'. Returns email IDs, subjects, and snippets. Use get_email with a message_id to read the full content.

max_results.description: Maximum number of emails to return (default: 20, max: 100)

page_token.description: Token for pagination to get next page of results

function.name: gmail_get_email
function.description: Get the full content of a specific email by its message ID (obtained from list_emails). Returns subject, body, sender, recipients, and attachments info.

message_id.description: The ID of the email to retrieve

function.name: gmail_send_email
function.description: Send a new email via Gmail. Use this for email — for Telegram messages use send_message instead. To reply to an existing thread, use reply_email instead of this.

is_html.description: Whether the body is HTML (default: false)

function.name: gmail_reply_email
function.description: Reply to an existing email within the same thread. Use this instead of send_email when responding to a conversation.

message_id.description: ID of the message to reply to

is_html.description: Whether the body is HTML (default: false)

function.name: gmail_delete_email
function.description: Move an email to trash

message_id.description: ID of the message to delete

function.name: gmail_mark_read
function.description: Mark an email as read or unread

read.description: true to mark as read, false to mark as unread

function.name: gmail_modify_labels
function.description: Add or remove labels from an email. Accepts label IDs or label names — names are automatically resolved to IDs.

add_label_ids.description: Label IDs or names to add

remove_label_ids.description: Label IDs or names to remove

function.name: gmail_get_new_emails
function.description: Get emails that have arrived since the last poll (requires polling to be enabled). Returns from an in-memory queue.

clear.description: Clear the queue after reading (default: false)

function.name: gmail_list_drafts
function.description: List all email drafts

function.name: gmail_create_draft
function.description: Create a new email draft

is_html.description: Whether the body is HTML (default: false)

function.name: gmail_update_draft
function.description: Update an existing email draft

draft_id.description: ID of the draft to update

is_html.description: Whether the body is HTML (default: false)

function.name: gmail_send_draft
function.description: Send an existing draft

draft_id.description: ID of the draft to send

function.name: gmail_delete_draft
function.description: Delete a draft

draft_id.description: ID of the draft to delete

function.name: gmail_list_labels
function.description: List all Gmail labels (both system and user-created)

function.name: gmail_create_label
function.description: Create a new Gmail label

function.name: gmail_delete_label
function.description: Delete a Gmail label (only user-created labels can be deleted)

label_id.description: ID of the label to delete

function.name: gmail_list_attachments
function.description: List all attachments for a specific email

message_id.description: ID of the message to list attachments for

function.name: gmail_get_attachment
function.description: Download an attachment (returns base64-encoded data)

message_id.description: ID of the message containing the attachment

attachment_id.description: ID of the attachment to download

function.name: gmail_list_calendars
function.description: List all calendars the user has access to, including shared calendars

function.name: gmail_list_events
function.description: List calendar events within a date range. Returns event summaries with id, title, times, organizer, and attendeeCount. For full attendee details, call get_event. Defaults to primary calendar.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID to list events from (default: "primary")

max_results.description: Maximum number of events to return (default: 25, max: 250)

function.name: gmail_get_event
function.description: Get full event details by ID, including attendee names, emails, and RSVP status.

event_id.description: The ID of the event to retrieve

function.name: gmail_create_event
function.description: Create a new calendar event. For timed events use start_date_time/end_date_time (ISO 8601). For all-day events use start_date/end_date (YYYY-MM-DD).

end_date.description: End date for all-day events (YYYY-MM-DD, exclusive)

function.name: gmail_update_event
function.description: Update an existing calendar event. Only provide fields you want to change. Use response_status to accept, decline, or tentatively accept an invite.

event_id.description: ID of the event to update

attendees.description: Updated list of attendee email addresses

function.name: gmail_delete_event
function.description: Delete a calendar event

event_id.description: ID of the event to delete

function.name: gmail_quick_add_event
function.description: Create a calendar event using natural language (e.g., "Meeting with John tomorrow at 3pm for 1 hour")

text.description: Natural language description of the event to create

function.name: gmail_find_free_time
function.description: Query free/busy information for one or more calendars within a time range. Returns busy time blocks.

time_min.description: Start of time range to check (ISO 8601)

time_max.description: End of time range to check (ISO 8601)

function.name: gmail_list_filters
function.description: List all Gmail filters/rules

function.name: gmail_get_filter
function.description: Get a specific Gmail filter by ID

filter_id.description: The ID of the filter to retrieve

function.name: gmail_create_filter
function.description: Create a new Gmail filter/rule. Criteria defines which emails match, action defines what happens to them.

size_comparison.description: Match larger or smaller than size

action.description: What to do with matching emails

function.name: gmail_delete_filter
function.description: Delete a Gmail filter by ID

filter_id.description: The ID of the filter to delete

function.name: telegram_send_message
function.description: Send a text message via Telegram. Use this for Telegram messaging — for email use send_email instead. Accepts chat_id as '@username' or numeric ID. Use list_chats first if you need to find the right chat.

chat_id.description: Chat ID or username to send message to (e.g., '@username' or numeric ID)

reply_to.description: Optional message ID to reply to

function.name: telegram_get_messages
function.description: Get recent message history from a specific Telegram chat. Returns messages in reverse chronological order. Use search_messages instead if you need to find messages matching specific text.

chat_id.description: Chat ID or username to get messages from

limit.description: Number of messages to retrieve (1-100, default 20)

offset_id.description: Get messages before this message ID (for pagination)

function.name: telegram_search_messages
function.description: Search Telegram messages by text query. Provide chat_id to search within one chat, or omit it to search globally across all chats.

chat_id.description: Chat ID to search in (omit for global search)

function.name: telegram_delete_messages
function.description: Delete messages from a chat (requires appropriate permissions)

chat_id.description: Chat ID where messages are located

message_ids.description: Array of message IDs to delete

function.name: telegram_list_chats
function.description: List all Telegram dialogs/chats including private chats, groups, and channels

limit.description: Maximum number of chats to retrieve (1-200, default 50)

function.name: telegram_get_chat
function.description: Get detailed information about a chat, user, or channel

chat_id.description: Chat ID or username (e.g., '@username' or numeric ID)

function.name: telegram_create_group
function.description: Create a new Telegram group chat with specified users

user_ids.description: Array of user IDs or usernames to add to the group

function.name: telegram_list_contacts
function.description: List all saved Telegram contacts

function.name: telegram_add_contact
function.description: Add a new contact to Telegram by phone number

function.name: telegram_search_users
function.description: Search for Telegram users globally by username or name

function.name: telegram_send_media
function.description: Send a photo or document to a Telegram chat. Accepts local file paths or URLs.

chat_id.description: Chat ID or username to send media to

file_path.description: Local file path or URL (http/https) to the image or file to send

function.name: telegram_download_media
function.description: Download media (photo, document, video) from a message

chat_id.description: Chat ID where the message is located

message_id.description: Message ID containing the media to download

output_path.description: Absolute path where to save the downloaded file

function.name: telegram_get_me
function.description: Get information about the current authenticated Telegram account

function.name: telegram_mark_read
function.description: Mark messages as read in a chat

chat_id.description: Chat ID to mark as read

message_id.description: Mark messages up to this ID as read (optional, marks all if omitted)

function.name: telegram_get_new_messages
function.description: Get new messages received since last call. Returns and clears the message queue unless peek=true.

peek.description: If true, return messages without clearing queue

function.name: telegram_subscribe_chat
function.description: Manage chat subscriptions for real-time messages. Subscribe to specific chats or receive all.

action.description: subscribe=add chat, unsubscribe=remove chat, list=show subscriptions, clear=receive all

function.name: codexec_execute_code
function.description: One-shot sandboxed code execution. Runs code in an isolated subprocess and returns stdout, stderr, exit code, and any generated artifacts.

working_dir.description: Working directory for execution (default: sandbox temp dir)

function.name: codexec_start_session
function.description: Start a persistent REPL session for interactive code execution. State is preserved across send_to_session calls.

function.name: codexec_send_to_session
function.description: Send code to an existing REPL session (or auto-create one). State is preserved between calls. Use for iterative development and data exploration.

session_id.description: Session ID from a previous start_session call. If omitted, a new session is created automatically.

language.description: Language for auto-created session. Required when session_id is omitted.

code.description: Code to execute in the session

function.name: codexec_close_session
function.description: Close a REPL session and free its resources

function.name: codexec_list_sessions
function.description: List all active REPL sessions with their status, language, and uptime

function.name: codexec_save_script
function.description: Save a reusable script to the script library. Scripts can be run later with run_script.

name.description: Script name (will be slugified for storage)

function.name: codexec_get_script
function.description: Get a saved script by name

function.name: codexec_list_scripts
function.description: List all saved scripts, optionally filtered by language or tag

function.name: codexec_search_scripts
function.description: Search saved scripts by keyword (matches name, description, tags)

function.name: codexec_run_script
function.description: Run a saved script by name. Installs required packages and executes in a sandbox.

function.name: codexec_save_and_run_script
function.description: Save a script to the library and immediately run it. Combines save_script + run_script in one call.

function.name: codexec_delete_script
function.description: Delete a saved script from the library

function.name: codexec_install_package
function.description: Install a package via pip (Python) or npm (Node). Can install globally or into a specific session.

session_id.description: Install into a specific session. If omitted, installs globally.

function.name: onepassword_list_vaults
function.description: List all 1Password vaults accessible to the service account

function.name: onepassword_list_items
function.description: List items in a 1Password vault with optional category filtering

vault.description: Vault name or ID. Use the vault name (e.g., 'Private') or ID from list_vaults response.

categories.description: Optional array to filter by item categories (e.g., 'Login', 'API Credential', 'SecureNote')

function.name: onepassword_get_item
function.description: Get full details of a 1Password item including all fields and their references

vault.description: Vault name or ID. Optional if item ID is globally unique.

function.name: onepassword_read_secret
function.description: Read a specific secret value using a 1Password secret reference URI (op://vault/item/field)

reference.description: 1Password secret reference URI in the format 'op://vault-name/item-name/field-name'

function.name: guardian_scan_content
function.description: Scan content for prompt injection attacks. Handles strings, objects, and arrays by recursively extracting all string values. Returns safe/unsafe verdict with confidence score and threat details.

content.description: Content to scan - can be a string, object, or array

source.description: Where the content came from: email, gmail, web, file, etc.

function.name: guardian_get_scan_log
function.description: Retrieve audit log of past security scans

scan_id.description: Get a specific scan by its ID

limit.description: Maximum number of scans to return (default: 50, max: 1000)

threats_only.description: Only return scans where threats were detected

function.name: web_browser_navigate
function.description: Navigate to a URL in the browser. Use this to open web pages for interaction (clicking, filling forms, logging in). For simply reading page content, prefer searcher_web_fetch instead.

function.name: web_browser_navigate_back
function.description: Navigate back to the previous page in browser history.

function.name: web_browser_snapshot
function.description: Capture an accessibility snapshot of the current page. Returns a structured representation of all interactive elements (links, buttons, inputs, text) with their ref IDs for use with browser_click and browser_type.

function.name: web_browser_click
function.description: Click an element on the page identified by its ref ID from browser_snapshot.

ref.description: Exact ref ID from the accessibility snapshot

function.name: web_browser_type
function.description: Type text into an editable element on the page identified by its ref ID from browser_snapshot.

ref.description: Exact ref ID from the accessibility snapshot

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to press Enter after typing (default: false)

function.name: web_browser_fill
function.description: Clear and fill an input element with new text. Unlike browser_type, this clears existing content first.

ref.description: Exact ref ID from the accessibility snapshot

value.description: Value to fill into the element

function.name: web_browser_take_screenshot
function.description: Take a screenshot of the current browser page. Returns a base64-encoded PNG image. Use this for visual verification or to capture page state.

function.name: web_browser_tab_list
function.description: List all open browser tabs with their titles and URLs.

function.name: web_browser_tab_new
function.description: Open a new browser tab, optionally navigating to a URL.

url.description: URL to navigate to in the new tab

function.name: web_browser_tab_select
function.description: Switch to a different browser tab by index.

index.description: Zero-based tab index to switch to

function.name: web_browser_tab_close
function.description: Close a browser tab by index. Closes the current tab if no index specified.

function.name: web_browser_close
function.description: Close the browser and release all resources.

function.name: web_browser_select_option
function.description: Select an option from a dropdown/select element on the page.

ref.description: Exact ref ID from the accessibility snapshot

function.name: web_browser_hover
function.description: Hover over an element on the page to trigger tooltips or dropdown menus.

ref.description: Exact ref ID from the accessibility snapshot

function.name: web_browser_drag
function.description: Drag an element to a target location on the page.

startElement.description: Human-readable description of the element to drag

startRef.description: Ref ID of the element to drag

endElement.description: Human-readable description of the drop target

endRef.description: Ref ID of the drop target

function.name: web_browser_press_key
function.description: Press a keyboard key or key combination (e.g., 'Enter', 'Escape', 'Control+a').

key.description: Key or key combination to press

function.name: web_browser_wait
function.description: Wait for a specified time before continuing. Use when pages need time to load dynamic content.

function.name: web_browser_pdf_save
function.description: Save the current page as a PDF file.

function.name: web_browser_console_messages
function.description: Get browser console messages (errors, warnings, logs) from the current page.

function.name: web_browser_file_upload
function.description: Upload a file to the page by providing a file path to an input element.

function.name: web_browser_network_requests
function.description: Get network requests made by the current page. Useful for debugging API calls and resource loading.

function.name: send_telegram
function.description: Send a message to a Telegram chat. The chat_id is auto-injected if omitted.

chat_id.description: The Telegram chat ID (auto-injected if omitted)

reply_to.description: Optional message ID to reply to

function.name: store_fact
function.description: Store a fact or learning about the user in memory

function.name: search_memories
function.description: Search for relevant memories and past conversations

function.name: get_status
function.description: Get the current status of the orchestrator, including available MCP servers, session info, and system health.

function.name: system_health_check
function.description: Run health checks on all connected MCP servers. Returns per-MCP availability and health status, classified as internal or external.

scope.description: Which MCPs to check: "all" (default), "internal" (auto-discovered), or "external" (from external-mcps.json).

function.name: get_tool_catalog
function.description: List available tools grouped by MCP server. Without mcp_name: returns a compact overview (MCP names + tool counts). With mcp_name: returns full tool names and descriptions for that MCP.

mcp_name.description: Filter to a specific MCP server name (e.g. "github", "vercel", "posthog"). Omit for all.

function.name: queue_task
function.description: Queue a task to run in the background immediately. Returns a task ID for tracking progress. Use get_job_status with the returned taskId to check completion.

name.description: Human-readable name for the task (e.g., "Send notification")

action.description: Action to execute in the background

type.description: Action type: "tool_call" for single tool, "workflow" for multiple steps

toolName.description: Exact tool name to call. Required for type=tool_call

parameters.description: Parameters to pass to the tool

function.name: get_job_status
function.description: Get the status of a background task queued via queue_task. Returns status (queued/running/completed/failed), timestamps, duration, result or error message.

function.name: trigger_backfill
function.description: Trigger a one-time conversation history backfill. Processes old conversations that were never mined for facts, extracting user information in batches. Progress and completion are reported via Telegram.

function.name: spawn_subagent
function.description: Spawn a temporary subagent to handle a task independently. The subagent runs as a separate Thinker process with its own LLM context. This tool blocks until the subagent finishes and returns the result. Use for parallelizable subtasks. Max 5 concurrent subagents per parent.

allowedTools.description: Restrict tools to these glob patterns (subset of parent's tools)

deniedTools.description: Additional tools to deny (on top of parent's denied list)

model.description: Override LLM model (default: inherit from parent)
